evals.question_short: Walk through phase 2 of sp2-pgstr-maxalloc implementation

assertions.text: Names the pre-phase spot-check of 3-5 file:line cites against current source (R2 / SKILL.md §Pre-phase)
Names the drift threshold (>10% / ~20 lines) that triggers a STOP + /pg-plan re-run
Mentions building incrementally after each edit with ninja install on dev/build-debug to catch compile errors immediately
Names the appropriate phase-end-check meson scopes (regress vs isolation vs recovery TAP) based on what the phase touches
States that phase-end check must pass BEFORE the commit (R4)
Names the required Plan: trailer format pointing to planning/<slug>/plan.md (phase N: <title>)
Names the Sites: trailer for non-obvious multi-file diffs
Explicitly notes NO Co-Authored-By because dev/ commits may be format-patched upstream (R5 / commit-message-style)
Names the notes.md append template with Status/Commit/Tests-run/What-changed/Surprises/What-this-phase-did-NOT-do (R8)
Cites the .claude/rules/pg-implement-discipline.md file by name or specific rule numbers (R1-R12)
Names R3 one-phase-at-a-time / no-interleaving as the gate before opening phase 3
Mentions verifying that changed files match the plan's §3 file table after tests pass (scope-creep gate)

evals.question_short: Mid-phase need to touch file outside plan §3

assertions.text: Names R7 (scope creep) as the governing rule
Lists all three resolution paths (small+coupled → update plan + Sites trailer; separate concern → defer to follow-up + notes.md; invalidates phase → escalate for re-plan)
States 'STOP editing' as the immediate first step rather than continuing silently
Names escalation to the user as required when the resolution isn't obviously path (1)
Requires the surprise be recorded in planning/<slug>/notes.md under 'Surprises / drift' (R8 template)
Requires the commit's Sites: trailer be updated if path (1) is taken
Names R10 two-repo separation: corpus fix goes in a SEPARATE meta-repo commit using meta-commit-style
Names R9 citation chain (commit → plan → corpus → source) as the integrity invariant that must hold
Explains the rationale: silent scope expansion breaks per-phase reviewability

evals.question_short: Trap: commit phase 3 broken with TODO and continue to phase 4

assertions.text: Names R4 (phase-end check must pass before commit) as the primary blocking rule
Names R3 (no interleaving; phase N must finish before phase N+1)
Names the anti-pattern list: WIP commits explicitly forbidden
Mentions R6 (vague language in commit messages forbidden — 'fix the bug' is the canonical bad example)
Mentions R12 (end-of-implementation gate requires full meson test green + exactly N commits for N phases)
Notes that dev/ commits may be format-patched upstream, so broken commits in the series block upstream submission
Cites bisectability of git history as a concrete harm
Cites reviewer experience (can't be expected to mentally skip a known-broken commit) as a concrete harm
Names the legitimate alternative: mark phase as 'partial' or 'deferred' in notes.md Status field per R8 template, then stop
Names escalation to the user as a SKILL.md §When to escalate trigger when a test fails for reasons not in plan §13 risks
